summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a high‑energy ‘88 Dead show with standout Foolish Heart, Althea, Let It Grow and a smoking Believe It Or Not.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the best of the 1988 Greek run – tight, high‑octane playing with Foolish Heart opening the set, monster versions of Althea, Let It Grow and an unforgettable Believe It Or Not. Reviewers rave about the full‑bodied matrix mix, Brent’s immaculate board work and a superb balance that lets every instrument shine, while only a few songs (Blackbird, Box) draw mild criticism.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 reviews uniformly rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and sound quality; only isolated minor song criticisms exist, giving a near‑perfect combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Premium matrix mix with crystal‑clear instrument separation, full vocal/keyboard blend, and Brent’s board work highlighted as “big‑fat full sound”. No dropout or distortion complaints.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rolls smoothly with a relaxed, West‑Coast vibe; the second set bursts with nonstop energy, delivering tight jams and memorable transitions (UJB → Victim → Crazy Fingers etc.).

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone described as “perfect” and his vocals praised on Candyman, Althea and Believe It Or Not; a few notes of vocal slip on Box but overall glowing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass Modulus highlighted as “big‑fat”; vocal contributions noted as solid, especially on Box and Victim.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s growl on Watchtower praised; occasional criticism for his heavy chord in Throwing Stones that masks other instruments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) receives repeated accolades for his board work and musical lifts on Big River, Let It Grow and overall mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s groove on Let It Grow noted as a highlight; drumming generally described as “tight” and “solid”.
